framework: A path qualifies only when >=3 prominent, proven people in the field visibly share the capability, each with a resolving link — the pattern across standouts is the evidence, not market demand. Ranked by strength of that pattern. Curated editorial, not DOL-derived.

paths.path: Become an embedded chief/in-house economist inside a tech company
paths.what_it_is: Apply econometrics, market design, and causal inference directly inside a tech company's product and policy decisions, often as a named economics lead.
paths.why_it_signals: It proves your research skills convert directly into decisions that move a real business, not just publications — the kind of applied judgment companies pay to keep in-house.

paths.path: Publish a public-facing economics newsletter or blog
paths.what_it_is: Write regularly for a public audience, translating economic analysis into clear, accessible commentary on a personal newsletter or blog.
paths.why_it_signals: Sustained public writing proves you can turn technical analysis into arguments non-economists actually read and trust — a skill few credentialed economists build.

paths.path: Turn research into an open, policy-used public data tool
paths.what_it_is: Build and maintain a large-scale, publicly accessible dataset or interactive tool that policymakers, journalists, and other researchers use directly.
paths.why_it_signals: It proves you can turn research into infrastructure other people build decisions on — impact that outlasts any single paper.
